medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Patient is a female with a history of hypertension and hyperlipidemia who had a syncopal episode at dinner last night, lasting 5 seconds. No post-ictal symptoms or confusion, and no pre-syncopal symptoms including nausea, diaphoresis, chest pain, shortness of breath, palpitations, or lightheadedness. No history of CAD or arrhythmias.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Hypertension, Hyperlipidemia, Diverticulosis, Hemorrhoids, Osteopenia, Pilonidal cyst removal.
medical_history.family_history: No history of sudden cardiac death. Her mother had thyroid, uterine, and stomach cancer; her father died of mesenteric ischemia and infarction, had coronary artery disease, and diabetes; her sister died of an unclear blood illness.

physical_examination.general_examination: T: 98.2, BP: 145/92, HR: 70, RR: 16, O2 97% RA. Pleasant, well appearing, NAD. No conjunctival pallor, no icterus, MMM, OP clear. Supple neck, no carotid bruits, no LAD, no JVD, no thyromegaly. RRR, normal S1, S2. II/VI SEM loudest at the LUSB. Lungs CTAB, good BS, no W/R/C. Abdomen soft, NT, ND, no HSM. Extremities warm, well perfused, no CCE. 2+ DP pulses. No rashes/lesions, ecchymoses.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological: AOx3, appropriate, cranial nerves grossly intact, preserved sensation throughout, strength throughout, equal reflexes. Ambulates without difficulty.
